ekadashis.name: Shattila Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains, beans, or lentils. Sesame (til) is especially sacred today — donate it, consume it, bathe with it, or offer it. Fruits, roots, milk, and nuts are permitted.
ekadashis.significance: One who observes this Ekadashi is freed from all sins. Sesame seeds are the main offering. Lord Vishnu is pleased and grants liberation.

ekadashis.name: Bhaimi (Jaya) Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: Strict fast — no grains, no beans. Water, fruits, milk, and nuts permitted. Vaishnava devotees avoid even onion and garlic.
ekadashis.significance: Observing Jaya Ekadashi frees one from the curse of becoming a ghost or evil spirit. It is one of the most important Ekadashis for liberation.

ekadashis.name: Vijaya Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, roots, milk, and nuts are permitted. One should worship Lord Vishnu all day and night if possible.
ekadashis.significance: This Ekadashi grants victory in all endeavors. Lord Rama observed this fast before crossing the ocean to Lanka. Bestows piety equal to horse sacrifice.

ekadashis.name: Amalaki Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship the Amalaki (Indian gooseberry) tree — it is considered Lord Vishnu's favorite. Fruits, milk, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Also known as Rangbhari Ekadashi. The Amalaki tree embodies Lord Vishnu. Observance grants results equal to a thousand Ashwamedha yajnas.

ekadashis.name: Papmochani Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains, no beans. Fruits, milk, nuts, and roots are permitted. Worship Lord Vishnu and offer tulsi leaves.
ekadashis.significance: Papmochani means 'destroyer of sins.' Even the gravest sins including Brahmahatya are destroyed by this fast. Grants liberation and purification of the soul.

ekadashis.name: Kamada Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, nuts are allowed. If possible, worship Lord Vishnu all night. Recite or hear the glories of this Ekadashi.
ekadashis.significance: Kamada means 'wish-fulfilling.' Destroys the worst sins including killing Brahmins. Frees one from the curse of becoming a Rakshasa. Grants all desires and moksha.

ekadashis.name: Varuthini Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains, legumes, or gambling. Avoid sleeping during the day, eating multiple times, or false speech. Fruits, milk, nuts permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Grants happiness in this world and liberation in the next. Equivalent to giving charity of 10,000 cows. Worshipping Lord Trivikrama is especially auspicious.

ekadashis.name: Mohini Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship Lord Vishnu in His Mohini form. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts are allowed. Observe brahmacharya.
ekadashis.significance: Removes all attachments and illusion (moha). Grants liberation and results superior to bathing in all holy rivers. Destroys even very serious sins instantly.

ekadashis.name: Apara Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, nuts, and roots permitted. Worship Lord Trivikrama. Avoid meat, fish, and intoxicants the day before.
ekadashis.significance: Apara means 'unlimited.' This fast bestows unlimited merit equal to performing many great sacrifices. Destroys the sins of false witness, killing cows, and abandoning one's duty.

ekadashis.name: Pandava Nirjala Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: Nirjala means complete fast — no food AND no water. Only achamana (sipping a tiny bit of water for purification) is permitted. This is the strictest Ekadashi fast.
ekadashis.significance: The most powerful Ekadashi. One Nirjala Ekadashi equals all 24 other Ekadashis combined. Bhima observed this since he could not fast on all other Ekadashis. Grants immense punya.

ekadashis.name: Yogini Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, nuts, and roots permitted. Feed Brahmins and devotees if possible. Worship Lord Vishnu.
ekadashis.significance: Destroys all sins including crimes against sages. Grants liberation. Merit equals feeding 88,000 Brahmins. One who keeps this fast is freed from all diseases.

ekadashis.name: Shayani Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Lord Vishnu goes to Yoga-nidra (cosmic sleep) on this day, beginning the Chaturmasya period. Only fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Also called Devshayani Ekadashi. Lord Vishnu reclines on Shesha Naga for 4 months. Marks the start of the sacred Chaturmasya period.

ekadashis.name: Kamika Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Tulsi leaves should be offered to Lord Vishnu today. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts are permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Observing this Ekadashi during Chaturmasya is especially powerful. Grants results of bathing in holy rivers. Destroys sins equal to killing an elephant.

ekadashis.name: Pavitropana Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship Lord Vishnu and offer a sacred thread (pavitra) made of cotton or gold. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Also called Putrada Ekadashi. Grants sons to the childless, fame, and moksha. Removes sins of this and past lives. Especially auspicious during Chaturmasya.

ekadashis.name: Annada Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted. Feed the poor and Brahmins if possible — anna (food) charity is especially auspicious today.
ekadashis.significance: Annada means 'giver of food.' Grants merit equal to giving unlimited food to the hungry. Destroys all sins and grants liberation.

ekadashis.name: Parsva Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Lord Vishnu turns on His side (parsva) during His cosmic sleep. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed. Worship Vamana Dev especially.
ekadashis.significance: Also called Vamana Ekadashi or Parivartini Ekadashi. Lord Vishnu turns over in His sleep. Great piety earned; results equal to feeding 1,000 Brahmins.

ekadashis.name: Indira Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Special benefit: offer pinda (rice balls) to one's ancestors if possible. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted.
ekadashis.significance: This Ekadashi rescues deceased ancestors from hellish planets. Especially sacred in Pitru Paksha.

ekadashis.name: Pasankusha Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts are permitted. Worship Lord Padmanabha (Vishnu). This fast should be observed very strictly.
ekadashis.significance: One of the greatest Ekadashis. Grants results equal to all forms of charity and pilgrimage combined. The devotee is freed from the cycle of birth and death.

ekadashis.name: Rama Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted. Worship Lord Hrishikesha. Kartika month makes this especially auspicious.
ekadashis.significance: Destroys all sins and grants liberation. Kartika is the most sacred month — this Ekadashi in Kartika is exceptionally powerful and beneficial.

ekadashis.name: Utthana Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Lord Vishnu awakens from His cosmic sleep today — blow conch shells, ring bells, and chant! Fruits, milk, roots, nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Also called Prabodhini Ekadashi or Dev-Uthani Ekadashi. Lord Vishnu wakes up. The 4-month Chaturmasya period ends. All auspicious ceremonies can resume.

ekadashis.name: Utpanna Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted. This is the birthday of Ekadashi — the personified deity who emerged to slay the demon Mura.
ekadashis.significance: The origin of all Ekadashis. On this day, Ekadashi Devi was born from the body of Lord Vishnu to slay the demon Mura. Grants unlimited merit and destroys all sins.

ekadashis.name: Mokshada Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed. Read or hear the Bhagavad Gita — it is especially meritorious today.
ekadashis.significance: Mokshada means 'giver of liberation.' Can free deceased ancestors from hell. The Bhagavad Gita was spoken on this Ekadashi. One of the most sacred Ekadashis of the year.

ekadashis.name: Saphala Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, nuts, coconut, lime, and milk permitted. Offer betel leaves, incense, lamps, and flowers to Lord Vishnu.
ekadashis.significance: Saphala means 'fruitful.' Makes all endeavors successful. Destroys all sins and grants the same merit as performing a thousand Ashwamedha sacrifices.

ekadashis.name: Putrada Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted. Worship Lord Narayana and pray for good progeny and family welfare.
ekadashis.significance: Putrada means 'giver of sons/good children.' Grants pious progeny, removes childlessness, and ensures family lineage. Highly recommended for those desiring children.

ekadashis.name: Shattila Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains, beans, or lentils. Sesame (til) is especially sacred. Fruits, roots, milk, and nuts are permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Freed from all sins. Lord Vishnu grants liberation. Six acts with sesame seeds on this day purify the soul completely.

ekadashis.name: Bhaimi (Jaya) Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: Strict fast — no grains, no beans. Fruits, milk, and nuts permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Frees one from the curse of becoming a ghost or evil spirit. Grants liberation even to those who observe it accidentally.

ekadashis.name: Vijaya Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, roots, milk, and nuts are permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Grants victory in all endeavors. Lord Rama observed this fast before crossing the ocean to Lanka.

ekadashis.name: Amalaki Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship the Amalaki tree. Fruits, milk, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: The Amalaki tree embodies Lord Vishnu. Results equal to a thousand Ashwamedha yajnas.

ekadashis.name: Papmochani Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains, no beans. Fruits, milk, nuts, and roots are permitted. Worship Lord Vishnu and offer tulsi leaves.
ekadashis.significance: Destroyer of sins. Even the gravest sins are destroyed by this fast. Grants liberation and purification of the soul.

ekadashis.name: Kamada Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, nuts are allowed. Worship Lord Vishnu all night.
ekadashis.significance: Wish-fulfilling Ekadashi. Destroys the worst sins. Frees one from the curse of becoming a Rakshasa. Grants all desires and moksha.

ekadashis.name: Varuthini Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains, legumes, or gambling. Avoid sleeping during the day. Fruits, milk, nuts permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Grants happiness and liberation. Equivalent to giving 10,000 cows in charity.

ekadashis.name: Mohini Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship Lord Vishnu in His Mohini form. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Removes all attachments and illusion. Grants liberation superior to bathing in all holy rivers.

ekadashis.name: Apara Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, nuts, and roots permitted. Worship Lord Trivikrama.
ekadashis.significance: Bestows unlimited merit. Destroys the sins of false witness and killing cows. Results equal to performing many great sacrifices.

ekadashis.name: Pandava Nirjala Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: Complete fast — no food AND no water (nirjala). Only achamana is permitted. The strictest and most powerful Ekadashi fast.
ekadashis.significance: One Nirjala Ekadashi equals all 24 other Ekadashis combined. Grants immense punya and liberation.

ekadashis.name: Yogini Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, nuts, and roots permitted. Feed Brahmins and devotees if possible.
ekadashis.significance: Destroys all sins. Merit equals feeding 88,000 Brahmins. Frees one from all diseases.

ekadashis.name: Shayani Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Lord Vishnu enters Yoga-nidra. Begin Chaturmasya vows.
ekadashis.significance: Lord Vishnu reclines on Shesha Naga for 4 months. Marks the start of the sacred Chaturmasya period.

ekadashis.name: Kamika Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Tulsi leaves should be offered. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts are permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Especially powerful during Chaturmasya. Grants results of bathing in holy rivers.

ekadashis.name: Pavitropana Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Offer a sacred thread (pavitra) to Lord Vishnu. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Grants sons to the childless, fame, and moksha. Removes sins of this and past lives.

ekadashis.name: Annada Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted. Feed the poor — anna charity is especially auspicious.
ekadashis.significance: Giver of food. Grants merit equal to giving unlimited food to the hungry. Destroys all sins and grants liberation.

ekadashis.name: Parsva Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Lord Vishnu turns on His side. Worship Vamana Dev. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Also called Parivartini Ekadashi. Lord Vishnu turns over in His sleep. Results equal to feeding 1,000 Brahmins.

ekadashis.name: Indira Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Offer pinda to ancestors if possible. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Rescues deceased ancestors from hellish planets. Especially sacred in Pitru Paksha.

ekadashis.name: Pasankusha Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship Lord Padmanabha very strictly. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: One of the greatest Ekadashis. Grants results equal to all forms of charity and pilgrimage. Devotee freed from cycle of birth and death.

ekadashis.name: Rama Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship Lord Hrishikesha. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Destroys all sins and grants liberation. Kartika month makes this Ekadashi exceptionally powerful.

ekadashis.name: Utthana Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Lord Vishnu awakens today. Blow conch, ring bells, and chant! Fruits, milk, roots, nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Also called Prabodhini Ekadashi or Dev-Uthani Ekadashi. Lord Vishnu wakes up. Chaturmasya ends. All auspicious ceremonies can resume.

ekadashis.name: Utpanna Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted. Birthday of Ekadashi Devi.
ekadashis.significance: The origin of all Ekadashis. Ekadashi Devi was born from Lord Vishnu to slay the demon Mura. Grants unlimited merit.

ekadashis.name: Mokshada Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Read or hear the Bhagavad Gita. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Giver of liberation. Can free deceased ancestors from hell. The Bhagavad Gita was spoken on this Ekadashi.

ekadashis.name: Saphala Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, nuts, coconut, lime, and milk permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Makes all endeavors successful. Destroys all sins and grants merit equal to a thousand Ashwamedha sacrifices.

ekadashis.name: Putrada Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship Lord Narayana for good progeny. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Giver of pious children. Grants progeny, removes childlessness, and ensures family lineage.

ekadashis.name: Shattila Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains, beans, or lentils. Sesame (til) is especially sacred. Fruits, roots, milk, and nuts are permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Freed from all sins. Lord Vishnu grants liberation.

ekadashis.name: Bhaimi (Jaya) Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: Strict fast — no grains, no beans. Fruits, milk, and nuts permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Frees one from becoming a ghost or evil spirit. Grants liberation even to those who observe it accidentally.

ekadashis.name: Vijaya Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, roots, milk, and nuts are permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Grants victory in all endeavors. Lord Rama observed this fast before crossing the ocean to Lanka.

ekadashis.name: Amalaki Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship the Amalaki tree. Fruits, milk, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: The Amalaki tree embodies Lord Vishnu. Results equal to a thousand Ashwamedha yajnas.

ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains, no beans. Fruits, milk, nuts, and roots are permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Destroyer of sins. Even the gravest sins are destroyed. Grants liberation.

ekadashis.name: Kamada Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, nuts are allowed. Worship Lord Vishnu all night.
ekadashis.significance: Wish-fulfilling Ekadashi. Destroys the worst sins. Grants all desires and moksha.

ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains, legumes, or gambling. Fruits, milk, nuts permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Grants happiness and liberation. Equivalent to giving 10,000 cows in charity.

ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship Lord Vishnu in His Mohini form. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Removes all attachments and illusion. Grants liberation superior to bathing in all holy rivers.

ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, nuts, and roots permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Bestows unlimited merit equal to performing many great sacrifices. Destroys sins of false witness.

ekadashis.name: Pandava Nirjala Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: Complete fast — no food AND no water (nirjala). Only achamana is permitted.
ekadashis.significance: One Nirjala Ekadashi equals all 24 other Ekadashis. Grants immense punya and liberation.

ekadashis.name: Yogini Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, nuts, and roots permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Destroys all sins. Merit equals feeding 88,000 Brahmins. Frees one from all diseases.

ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Lord Vishnu enters Yoga-nidra (cosmic sleep). Begin Chaturmasya vows.
ekadashis.significance: Lord Vishnu reclines on Shesha Naga for 4 months. Marks start of Chaturmasya.

ekadashis.name: Kamika Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Offer tulsi leaves. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts are permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Especially powerful during Chaturmasya. Grants results of bathing in holy rivers.

ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Offer a sacred thread (pavitra) to Lord Vishnu. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Grants sons to the childless, fame, and moksha. Removes sins of this and past lives.

ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted. Feed the poor and Brahmins.
ekadashis.significance: Giver of food. Grants merit equal to giving unlimited food to the hungry. Destroys all sins.

ekadashis.significance: Also called Parivartini Ekadashi. Results equal to feeding 1,000 Brahmins.

ekadashis.name: Indira Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Offer pinda to ancestors if possible. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Rescues deceased ancestors from hellish planets. Especially sacred in Pitru Paksha.

ekadashis.name: Pasankusha Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship Lord Padmanabha very strictly. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: One of the greatest Ekadashis. Grants results equal to all forms of charity and pilgrimage.

ekadashis.significance: Prabodhini / Dev-Uthani Ekadashi. Lord Vishnu wakes up. Chaturmasya ends. All auspicious ceremonies can resume.

ekadashis.name: Utpanna Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Birthday of Ekadashi Devi. Celebrate with chanting and reading scripture.
ekadashis.significance: Origin of all Ekadashis. Ekadashi Devi was born from Lord Vishnu to slay demon Mura. Grants unlimited merit.

ekadashis.name: Mokshada Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Read or hear the Bhagavad Gita. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts allowed.
ekadashis.significance: Giver of liberation. Can free deceased ancestors from hell. The Bhagavad Gita was spoken on this Ekadashi.

ekadashis.name: Saphala Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Fruits, nuts, coconut, lime, and milk permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Makes all endeavors successful. Grants merit equal to a thousand Ashwamedha sacrifices.

ekadashis.name: Putrada Ekadashi
ekadashis.fasting_rules: No grains or legumes. Worship Lord Narayana for good progeny. Fruits, milk, roots, and nuts permitted.
ekadashis.significance: Giver of pious children. Grants progeny, removes childlessness, and ensures family lineage.
